# ID-27: session-driver-cmux maintenance — close S61 carryover script gaps

Close the 4 cmux-script gaps surfaced in S61 parallel-work (worktree-baseline-investigation, workspace:4) carryover #2 so cmux can fully replace Agent-tool isolation:worktree as the canonical parallel-dispatch primitive while two top-level worktrees persist.

Motivating finding: S61 confirmed Anthropic's Agent-tool isolation:worktree places sub-agents under the PRIMARY top-level worktree regardless of orchestrator CWD; S62 W1 empirical re-test (post user-global `baseRef:"head"` flip) re-confirmed STRUCTURAL not session-cache. cmux explicit `git worktree add` from PROJECT_ROOT bypasses this. With these 4 script gaps closed, cmux becomes the production-ready substitute and the parallel-session pattern can eventually collapse to single top-level worktree (Path A, ID-24) without losing parallelism.

Not blocking ID-9 / ID-20 work. Independent maintenance Task. ID-24 (Worktree collapse) remains blocked on ID-9 + ID-20 per Liam ratification (script fixes alone insufficient — substantive carry-forward work still needs to land on main first).

## What shipped (all 4 in one commit)

Commit `25a5fa1b` feat(session-driver-cmux): close S61 carryover script gaps

- launch-worker.sh `--brief <file>` flag — copies brief into worker as `.cmux-brief.md`, auto-sends "Read .cmux-brief.md before any work." after session_start; mirrors OQ-escalation channel shape.
- launch-worker.sh `.worktreeinclude` honour — literal-path copy on `git worktree add`; canonical case `.env.local`.
- stop-worker.sh `--delete-branch` opt-in flag — deletes worker branch post worktree removal; default OFF preserves cherry-pick window.
- wait-for-fleet.sh bash 3.2 portability — flat-string + awk lookup replaces `declare -A` associative arrays; macOS default /bin/bash 3.2.57 verified.

SKILL.md table updated; 2 Known-limitations rows dropped (now closed).

## OQs ratified inline (no spec chain)

- sdc-update OQ #3 (branch-cleanup canonical): opt-in flag on stop-worker (default off, parent passes after cherry-pick confirmed).
- launch-worker brief-delivery: auto-send pointer prompt on session_start (vs orchestrator-composes).
- ID-24 timing: keep blocked on ID-9 + ID-20.

Deferred (out of scope, not blocking): 3 oq-spec PRODUCT-invariant OQs (Q1/Q2/Q3) — ratify when authoring `docs/specs/oq-escalation/TECH.md`.

## Acceptance criteria — all met

- All 4 scripts syntax-PASS on /bin/bash 3.2.57.
- End-to-end live cmux dispatch from production-readiness orchestrator: worker landed in P-R `.claude/worktrees/` (NOT primary), `.cmux-brief.md` copied, `.env.local` copied via `.worktreeinclude`, auto-prompt emitted user_prompt_submit event, `wait-for-fleet.sh --timeout 3` cleanly produced Completed/Pending output + exit 1, `stop-worker.sh --force --delete-branch` removed worktree + branch + events dir.
- SKILL.md reflects new flags + drops closed gaps.

## Dependencies

None (ratification absorbed inline; no spec chain).

## Subtasks

### ID-27.1: wait-for-fleet.sh — bash 3.2 portability

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Replace `declare -A` associative arrays (AFTER_LINE, COMPLETED) with bash-3.2-portable flat strings (newline-delimited `sid=n` records + awk lookup; space-bordered `" sid1 sid2 "` membership-check string + explicit counter).

**Test strategy:** /bin/bash -n syntax check + live --timeout 3 against active SID produces Completed/Pending output with exit 1.

**Details:**

<info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline (no Executor dispatch — single-file bounded edit). Replaced `declare -A AFTER_LINE` with `AFTER_LINE_DATA` newline-delimited string + `after_line_for()` helper using awk lookup. Replaced `declare -A COMPLETED` with `COMPLETED_LIST` space-bordered string (case-pattern membership) + `COMPLETED_COUNT` integer counter. Trailing timeout-error message uses awk `{$1=$1;print}` whitespace trim for clean display. Verified `/bin/bash --version` shows 3.2.57(1) on macOS Darwin 25.2.0; `/bin/bash -n` passes; live `--timeout 3` test against an active session emitted `Error: timeout after 3s (mode=any)` + `Completed: <none>` + `Pending: <SID>` + exit 1. Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>

### ID-27.2: stop-worker.sh — `--delete-branch` opt-in flag

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Add `--delete-branch` flag (default OFF). After worktree removal, deletes the worker branch recorded in meta. Default-off preserves cherry-pick window for the parent orchestrator. Branch name sourced from meta's `branch` field (populated by launch-worker.sh).

**Test strategy:** Launch worker, immediately stop with --force --delete-branch; verify worktree gone + branch deleted + events dir gone.

**Details:**

<info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline. Flag parser refactored from positional `${3:-}` check to a while-loop accepting `--force` and `--delete-branch` in any order. Meta-file load gained `BRANCH_NAME` field. New section after worktree removal: skips if branch name empty (with warning), skips if branch already gone (note), otherwise `git branch -D` with warning on failure. sdc-update OQ #3 ratified: opt-in flag (vs always parent-handled) — centralised one-call cleanup, default-off preserves cherry-pick option. Live test: `stop-worker.sh sdc-fix-verify <SID> --force --delete-branch` removed worktree + branch + events dir; verified `git branch | grep cmux-worker-sdc-fix-verify` returned nothing post-call. Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>

### ID-27.3: launch-worker.sh — `--brief <file>` flag with auto-prompt

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

Add `--brief <file>` flag that copies a brief file into the worker worktree as `.cmux-brief.md` and auto-sends 'Read .cmux-brief.md before any work.' after session_start. Mirrors OQ-escalation channel shape (file-on-disk + structured pointer prompt). brief_path exposed in result JSON + meta file.

**Test strategy:** Launch with --brief, verify .cmux-brief.md exists in worker worktree + brief_path in result JSON + user_prompt_submit event in events.jsonl post session_start.

**Details:**

<info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline. Flag added to parser between `--branch` and EXTRA_ARGS. Brief existence validated pre-side-effects (early exit if file not found). Copy happens post `git worktree add` with rollback on cp failure. Auto-prompt sent after session_start detection via cmux send + send-key enter — orchestrator's downstream send-prompt.sh calls can assume brief was read. Meta file + result JSON both gain `brief_path` field. Live test: launched with `--brief /tmp/claude-501/sdc-fix-verify/brief.md` (617 bytes); worker worktree contained `.cmux-brief.md` (617 bytes preserved), events.jsonl showed `user_prompt_submit` event consistent with the auto-prompt firing, followed by 6 `pre_tool_use` events indicating the worker actively tooling on the brief steps. Ratified design: auto-send on boot (vs orchestrator-composes). Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>

### ID-27.4: launch-worker.sh — `.worktreeinclude` honour

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** _none_
- **Updated:** _unset_

After `git worktree add`, read `<project-root>/.worktreeinclude` (if exists) as a list of literal file paths (one per line, `#` comments skipped, blanks skipped). Copy each existing source from PROJECT_ROOT into the new worktree, preserving nested-path parent directories. Plain paths only — no glob expansion (kept minimal; extend if patterns needed). Canonical case: `.env.local`.

**Test strategy:** Launch worker, verify `.env.local` (or whichever paths .worktreeinclude lists) present in worker worktree.

**Details:**

<info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline alongside the --brief flag. Read loop strips CR (for CRLF files), awk-trims whitespace, skips blank + `#`-comment lines. Each path tested with `[ -e ]`; if present, `mkdir -p` the destination parent and `cp -R` source to destination with a warning on failure (non-fatal). Anthropic's `.worktreeinclude` mechanism is internal to `claude --worktree` / Agent-tool isolation / EnterWorktree — cmux's raw `git worktree add` bypasses it, so this is a deliberate mirror of the canonical case (`.env.local`) without claiming full pattern parity. Live test: `.worktreeinclude` at project root contained `.env.local` (1 line); worker worktree post-launch contained `.env.local` (1806 bytes preserved). Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>
